title: Report says Zambia needs more friendly tax regime to attract mining investors
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "The ZCM's report quotes research showing that mining investment in Zambia benefits not just the mining industry but also the wider economy, through what is known as the 'multiplier effect'. Chishimba explains that this means mines procure supplies from local businesses, and employees spend their wages in the economy, stimulating more business creation and more employment." | "a recent World Bank study on Canadian base metals producer First Quantum Minerals' Kansanshi mine, in the north-western province town of Solwezi found that for every direct employment opportunity created at the mine, a further five were created in the wider economy." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "Chishimba explains that this means mines procure supplies from local businesses, and employees spend their wages in the economy, stimulating more business creation and more employment." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "He remarks that, along with a decline in production, there will be a decline in government revenue, mining industry jobs and foreign exchange." | "From 1997, investors in the newly reprivatised Zambian mining industry collectively invested more than $12-billion in modernisation, expansion and new greenfield ventures." | "Importantly, this growth started before the copper price began to recover in 2004, proving it was the surge in investment which turned around the economic fortunes of the country," || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "Zambia needs a more competitive mining tax regime to entice new and existing investors to invest billions of dollars in the mining industry and boost flagging production, says Zambia Chamber of Mines (ZCM) president Nathan Chishimba." | "Zambia's overall effective mining tax rate remains among the highest in the world." | "The DRC's tax regime is not only more investor friendly than Zambia's, but has also been much more stable. title: Zambia : Mining company dumps large amounts of sulphur in Kafue river affecting water supply
impact_evidence: Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "Water supply to some Copperbelt towns has been cut off following discharge of sulphur into Kafue River by a Chingola-based mining company." | "The water utility company was forced to shut down the water treatment plant due to elevated levels of sulphates in Kafue River" || Environmental | Water Resources | Freshwater ecotoxicity (heavy metals, pH changes, sensitive species) -> "The water utility company was forced to shut down the water treatment plant due to elevated levels of sulphates in Kafue River which has even affected the fish." || Environmental | Water Resources | Water management challenges -> "NWSC Treatment plant was forced to shut down following the reported discharge of sulphur into Kafue River where drinking water is drawn and the move has created erratic water supply to Kitwe, Kalulushi and Chambishi towns." | "Some mining companies have made it a habit of discharging sulphur into the Kafue River forcing NWSC to shut down water supply" || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> "warning that mining companies and others who were discharging poisonous effluents into the river were playing with people's lives and should be heavily punished." | "On account of the erratic water supply following the shut down after a mining company discharged sulphur into Kafue River, many people were inconvenienced." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Mr Kabwe said Government through Zambia Environmental Management Agency (ZEMA) would ensure that mining companies that were discharging acid or sulphur in the rivers were heavily punished for their actions."

title: Zambia : No retrenchments at Ndola lime until all concersn are addressed - Lusambo
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "Copperbelt Minister Bowman Lusambo has directed that the impending retirement of around 140 workers at Ndola Lime Limited in Ndola be put on hold." | "Mr Lusambo has since assured workers at Ndola Lime Limited to continue working as Government is intervening in the matter." || Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "He said concerns from all stakeholders including the Mine Workers Union of Zambia should be taken on board before any decision is made." | "The moment this information was bought to the attention of my office as Copperbelt Minister, I immediately phoned the CEO at ZCCM-IH and the Director at Ndola Lime and directed them that no further action is taken until we meet and agree on how we can address the issues surrounding this matter," he said." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "Mr Lusambo directed that all steps have to be followed in line with the country's labour laws if any retrenchments are to be effected." | "He has also cautioned employers to desist from retrenching workers especially around the festive period and ensure that everything else is done to mitigate the situation if such layoffs are to effected."
